prompt: The method should insert an element at position k and shift right, but it corrupts the array. What is the bug?
choices: arr[k] = val should be placed before the loop
Shifting left-to-right overwrites arr[k] before it can be copied to arr[k+1]; the loop must iterate from size down to k+1
size should be arr.length, not a separate parameter
The loop should start at k + 1, not k
explanation: Iterating left-to-right: arr[k+1]=arr[k], arr[k+2]=arr[k+1] (already overwritten by arr[k]), etc. Every element from k onward becomes arr[k]. Fix: iterate right-to-left: for (int i = size-1; i >= k; i--) arr[i+1] = arr[i];
